name: Blowfish
creator: Bruce Schneier
breakability: No practical break of the full cipher is known, but it is considered outdated for new systems.
modern_use: Legacy software, older storage systems, and historical interoperability cases.
description: Blowfish is a symmetric block cipher designed as a fast, license-free alternative to older standards. It uses a 64-bit block size, which is the main reason it is no longer recommended for new designs.
notes: The small 64-bit block size makes Blowfish a poor fit for modern high-volume encryption. AES is the preferred replacement.
did_you_know: Blowfish was once widely used in security tools because it was fast, public, and free to implement.
